vocab.meaning: khả năng cảm thụ thẩm mỹ
vocab.example: Engaging with oil paints as a child helped develop my aesthetic sensibilities from a very early age.

vocab.meaning: một phương thức để giải tỏa sức sáng tạo
vocab.example: Sketching served as a vital creative outlet for me, especially during the more stressful periods of primary school.

vocab.meaning: kỹ năng vận động tinh (sự khéo léo của đôi tay)
vocab.example: My parents encouraged me to draw precisely to hone my fine motor skills and hand-eye coordination.
I experimented with every artistic medium available, from messy charcoal to vibrant watercolors.

vocab.meaning: nắm vững những nguyên tắc cơ bản
vocab.example: I spent several years attending weekend classes to master the fundamentals of perspective and shading.
Learning to paint was a lesson in discipline; it really helped me cultivate patience through trial and error.

vocab.meaning: sự tự do trong việc thể hiện bản thân
vocab.example: Our art teacher was wonderful because she granted us total expressive freedom without rigid rules.
As a young kid, I was obsessed with visual storytelling, often drawing long comic strips in my notebooks.

vocab.meaning: khả năng quan sát nhạy bén
vocab.example: Painting still-life objects at school forced me to develop a keen sense of observation for light and texture.

vocab.meaning: sự tự biểu đạt (của bản thân)
vocab.example: Drawing provided a powerful means of self-expression when words weren't enough to convey my thoughts.
